revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:35, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]]}} [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]] [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[sn:enjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]] [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]]{{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]][[Category:qq]]

In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]]}} [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]] [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[sn:enjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]] [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]]{{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]]}} [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]] [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[sn:enjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]] [[sn:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]]{{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tenjinyama Kofun (Kōfu)]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:35, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:35, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:35, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q60989898]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
