Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q60989898}}

The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]

The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * [[:en:1951|1951]]-[[:en:1953|1953]]: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * [[:en:2009|2009]]: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in [[:en:2015|2015]]. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]

The mound is in the [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]].Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Daimaruyama Kofun]] (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]], [[:en:Ōmaruyama Kofun|Ōmaruyama Kofun]], and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun]] (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], but no [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been found. Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. The mound is in the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}.Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Daimaruyama Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}, and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * {{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951}}-{{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}}: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * {{ill|2009|en|2009|WD=Q1996|lt=2009}}: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}}. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. revision.text:
[[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. The mound is in the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}.Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Daimaruyama Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}, and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * {{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951}}-{{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}}: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * {{ill|2009|en|2009|WD=Q1996|lt=2009}}: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015}}. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. The mound is in the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}.Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Daimaruyama Kofun|1=Ōmaruyama Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun|1=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}, and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] == Excavation History == * {{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}-{{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * {{ill|2009|en|2009|WD=Q1996|lt=2009|1=2009}}: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}}. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

However, Haji pottery imitating early {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] }} [[ja:天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:天神山古墳 (甲府市)]] {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q60989898}} [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments; consolidate 8 interlanguage link(s) into wikidata link template (has duplicate-language pairs) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24926181992|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tenjinyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tenjin-yama Kofun (Kofu), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound |alternate_name = Tenjinzuka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Shimomukoyama Town |coordinates = {{Coord|35.582925|N|138.580222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} |area = Mound length: 132 m
Height: 12 m (rear circular portion) |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} |Associated Tumuli = |built = Early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Kofu City Designated Historic Site: Tenjinyama Kofun |Tangible Cultural Property = |notes = Second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture |map = Japan Yamanashi |type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-north |map_dot_label = Tenjinyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} The '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (天神山古墳, also known as Tenjinzuka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. The mound is in the {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} type and has been designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site. This kofun is the second largest in Yamanashi Prefecture, following the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}.Major kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture include: 1. {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: 169 m. 2. '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' (Kofu City, Shimomukoyama Town) - Mound length: 132 m. 3. {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Daimaruyama Kofun|1=Ōmaruyama Kofun}} (Kofu City, Shimosone Town) - Mound length: approx. 120 m? It is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == Overview == The Tenjinyama Kofun is a large {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} built on a hill to the east of the Yonekura Mountains in central Yamanashi Prefecture. In addition to this kofun, other prominent kofun in the area include the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Ōmaruyama Kofun|en|Ōmaruyama Kofun|WD=Q11431880|lt=Ōmaruyama Kofun|1=Ōmaruyama Kofun}}, and {{ill|Kodairazawa Kofun|ja|小平沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodairazawa Kofun|qq=}}. Recent excavations have been conducted in this region. The mound is oriented north-south, with the front part facing north.{{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} It measures 132 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture after the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} (169 meters).{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The surface of the mound shows evidence of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been found. However, Haji pottery imitating early {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} was discovered.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} Surrounding the mound, a moat is believed to have existed.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The burial facility has not been identified.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} The kofun is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The kofun site was designation1 a Kofu City Historic Site in 2009 (Heisei 21).{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/somu-somu/shise/documents/1315_06.pdf|title=Kofu City Board of Education Notification No. 2|publisher=Kofu City Official Gazette|date=April 6, 2009}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1951|en|1951|WD=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}}-{{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}}: Discovery of Haji pottery during farming activities.{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * {{ill|2009|en|2009|WD=Q1996|lt=2009|1=2009}}: Designated a Kofu City Historic Site on March 25. * Excavations were conducted, with the Kofu City Board of Education publishing a report in {{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015|1=2015}}. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows:{{Harvid|Tenjinyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}} * Total length: 132 meters * Rear circular portion: ''' Diameter: 67 meters ''' Height: 12 meters * Front rectangular portion: ''' Width: 29 meters ''' Height: 7 meters == Historic Site == === Kofu City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Tenjinyama Kofun''' - Designated on March 25, 2009. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|author=Miyazawa Kimio|year=1989|chapter=Tenjinyama Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|year=2015|title=Tenjinyama Kofun: First and Second Excavation Reports (Kofu City Cultural Property Research Report No. 78)|publisher=Kofu City Board of Education}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{citation needed|ja|天神山古墳|version=89534583|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|version=99995729|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q60989898|ja|天神山古墳 (甲府市)|ja|天神山古墳|ja|%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%94%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)}} [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
